response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable, broadly experienced engineer, but her background is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. Her primary expertise is TypeScript and Node.js for web and backend systems, with D/DLang as a secondary focus for performance-sensitive work. Java appears only peripherally in her skills list and briefly at one employer; Kotlin is entirely absent. There is no demonstrated exposure to the Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework, and no evidence of JVM internals knowledge. The National Instruments role is the closest analogue — she contributed to a C IDE — but that is C tooling, not JVM tooling, and it was a short engagement over a decade ago.
strengths: Genuine interest in developer tooling demonstrated across multiple contexts: built open-source test runners and assertion libraries, contributed to an IDE product at NI, and currently works on MCP code generators and agentic frameworks
Proven ability to work on complex, high-throughput systems at scale (millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent practice of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active knowledge sharing through workshops and conference talks
concerns: No substantive Java or Kotlin experience; listed Java is unsupported by any role description, and Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume
Zero exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM frameworks — the technical core of this position
No evidence of JVM internals knowledge, build tool integration (Gradle/Maven), or IDE platform development (IntelliJ Platform, Eclipse, LSP/language server work)

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling interest and track record
